arabic_text:
بَیت
هَذَا الَّذِي تَعْرِفُ الْبَطْحَاءُ وَطْأَتَهُ
وَالْبَيْتُ يَعْرِفُهُ وَالْحِلُّ وَالْحَرَمُ
وزن
english_text:
ONE VERSE (بَيت)
First Half — 6 words, 14 Syllables
Second Half — 7 words, 14 Syllables
15 Meter
alt_text: Arabic prosody diagram of one bayt (verse) from al-Farazdaq's qasida. The bayt is split into a 'First Half' (6 words / 14 syllables) and a 'Second Half' (7 words / 14 syllables); a central wazn (meter) value of 15 separates them. Music-staff icons under each half evoke the meter analogy.
notes: Prosodic analysis: the same al-Farazdaq line introduced in binder 34 ch153 used here to illustrate Arabic meter (15 syllables symmetric across the two hemistichs).
